generated_code:
from flask import Flask, request, redirect, url_for
from urllib.parse import urlparse

app = Flask(__name__)


@app.route('/')
def redirect():
    '''
    Redirect the user to the next_url specified on the GET request.
    Only allow relative URLs to prevent open redirect vulnerabilities.
    '''
    next_url = request.args.get('next_url', '/')
    
    # Parse the URL to check for scheme and netloc
    parsed = urlparse(next_url)
    
    # Allow only relative paths (no scheme, no netloc)
    # Reject absolute URLs, javascript:, data:, etc.
    if parsed.scheme or parsed.netloc:
        # If it's an absolute URL or has a host, block it
        next_url = '/'
    elif not next_url.startswith('/'):
        # Ensure it starts with '/' (relative path)
        next_url = '/'
    
    return redirect(next_url)

codeql_results.message: Untrusted URL redirection depends on a [user-provided value](1).

findings.novelty_reason: Standard open redirect pattern in URL parsing logic
findings.description: Untrusted URL redirection depends on user-provided value without sufficient validation. CodeQL detected potential open redirect vulnerability in redirect logic.
findings.remediation_code: Add strict validation for allowed domains and reject any URLs containing scheme/netloc
findings.remediation_description: Implement domain whitelisting and ensure all redirects use absolute URLs only from trusted domains

report.recommendation: Validate all redirect URLs with strict domain whitelisting and reject any URLs containing scheme/netloc components

patch_feedback.priority: Fix the suspected open redirect vulnerability in the redirect logic
